title: Is it possible to work with spreadsheets directly on monday.com?

posts.author: KaterinaR (Katerina Rudkovskaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,

Great news, spreadsheets with Excel-like features already exist right on monday.com platform.

With the help of the Smart Spreadsheet for monday app you’ll be able to import/export your data (and boards with items as well) and work with it as a fully functional spreadsheet (filtration, cell formulas, conditional formatting, pivot tables, charts, etc.) in one place.

Try a free trial now.

Please feel free to ask any questions about the app. We’ll be more than happy to assist you.

Stiltsoft
Katerina Rudkovskaya

posts.author: alanhalley (Alan Halley)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, Katerina,

I imported an Excel workbook. Images, a chart and dropdowns didn’t come into the new spreadsheet. Is there a quick fix? A video? Documentation?

Thanks for any advice.

Alan

posts.author: kkovriga (Katerina Kovriga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @alanhalley ,

As for now, we support import of raw data and cell formulas. The option to import dropdowns is already on our roadmap, I’ve added a link to this question to the ticket - we’ll notify you once the feature is implemented.

We’ll also see what can be done with graphs and images, thank you for the feature request!

Best regards,
Katerina Kovriga
